problem: "Lined up, waiting on 12C for takeoff. Upon advancing to full throttle, strong burning rubber smell filled the cabin. I was vigilant about my fuel mixture and RPM settings while taxiing." (4-12-2025)

Operated the overhead vent blower,  operation was normal, no smell from the vent air. problem: At startup the engine sounded abnormal, though all instruments were in the green. Taxi and runup were normal with a 100RPM mag drop on each mag. whie holding short, we noticed a slight RPM dip at idle, resolved when we added 100RPM. On takeoff the engine sounded louder than normal almost like a slight misfire. Issue persisted in climb and seemed to get worse when we walked the throttle back in cruise flight. Engine instruments styed in the green EGT normal during full flight.
action: Performed ground run, verified ran rough when not pointed into the wind, mag selection didn't make a difference. Seamed to clear up later in the run. Cyl# 2 and 4 EGT lower then the rest. Removed cowling, began checking clearance of Cyl#2 and 4 exhaust valves, then notice engine left side exhaust stud nuts to be missing. Inspected exhaust and studs, no defects note. Reinstalled Cyl#2&4 exhaust stacks with new hardware, Reassembled Cy#2&4 valve train as required. Reinstalled bottom cowl.

problem: Alternator failure; aircraft at Lake Havasu (KHII)
action: Removed engine cowling.  Inspected alternator.  Determined alternator needs to be replaced.  Removed alternator, P/N AL24-P70-3  S/N H-W072873   Installed new alternator P/N AL24-P70-3  S/N H-Z100660  Torqued all mounting hardware to spec, adjusted belt tension.  Operational check good.  Reinstalled engine cowling.

Troubleshot voltage regulator finding voltage out to field was low.  Installed known good voltage regulator verifying correct field voltage.  Removed new alternator and reinstalled original alternator.  Received one bad voltage regulator bad out of the parts room.  Installed new voltage regulator.  Operational check good.  Reinstalled engine cowling.

problem: Fastener is loose on air box housing
action: Tightened fastener on air box housing on lower engine cowling
Installed and cleaned upper and lower cowlings.

problem: after getting oil from line we noticed oil dripping from RH cowling. MX advised write-up
action: removed RH top engine cowl, inspected, found no source of oil leak. oil believed to have come from spilling while servicing RH engine. washed engine and cowling, ran a/c leak check good. installed top cowling.

problem: RH engine bottom cowl chafing on #1 EGT wiring.
action: applied chafe tape to RH bottom cowling, no damage to wiring found.

problem: During runup left mag check resulted in smooth RPM drop down to 1950 PRM but then began fluctuating erratically between 1850 RPM and 1950 RPM. Ran overly rick check but had no effect. Ran fouled plug check but had no effect.
action: Ran AC duplicated discrep. Removed top cowl removed bottom #1 and #3 spark plugs and top #2 and #4 spark plugs. Cleaned and tested plugs. Tested leads for those cylinders no defects noted. Reinstalled all plugs and leads. Ran AC problem still occurred. Removed and replaced RH mag with overhauled. PN: 4373 SN: 21110300. Ran AC no defects noted. Ops and leak check good. Reinstalled top cowl.

problem: L/H ENG BOTTOM COWLING FIRE BLANKET HAS A HOLE
action: PATCHED LH LOWER COWL FIRE BLANKET WITH NEW MATERIAL.
Started fabricating doublers for cracks in RH upper cowl

Finished fabrication of inner and outer doublers. Alodined and primed outer doubler. Riveted outer doubler on IAW AC 43.13-1B chpt 4-57. Installed inner doublers with rivets and henkel loctite 9309.3NA
masked off and painted repair white
Removed and replaced migrating camlock on upper cowl with new.

problem: pilot reports LH engine top cowling screw is loose.
action: tightened LH engine top cowling screw.

problem: CO level CAS indication on takeoff roll with a headwind, continued until vent open on climbout. PPM remained 0 for remainder of flight, highest PPM indicated 30.
action: ran a/c with handheld CO detector could not duplicate discrepancy, removed top cowl to inspect engine and exhaust, no defects found. Reinstalled top cowl.

problem: RH propeller won't return to low pitch after it is put into the feather position.
action: Removed RH top engine cowling.  Removed RH spinner, checked propeller dome pressure,  pressure was correct.  Checked accumulator pressure, no pressure.  Charged accumulator to 100 PSI, found leaks at the fitting.  Removed fitting and cleaned the threads.  Resealed fitting and reinstalled into accumulator.  Recharged accumulator to 100 PSI and tested with soapy water, no leaks noted.  Operational check good.  Reinstalled spinner.  Reinstalled top cowling.
Stop Drilled Cracks in the FWD NLG / Cowl Fairing and repaired with Two- Part Epoxy

Removed cowling. Performed 50 HR engine oil change. Inspected oil filter element with no defects noted. Serviced engine with 8 quarts of 15W50 oil. Inspected interior and exterior lights. Lubricated throttle and mixture control cable rod ends, and flight control hinges. Ran engine to temp and leak checked with none noted. Installed engine cowling.

problem: pilot reports camlock on front of bottom cowl keeps popping out.
action: removed and replaced camlock with new on air filter retainer fairing on bottom cowling.ops check good.

problem: lower cowl r/h rib has delamination.
action: cleaned up damaged section on the r/h rib on lower cowl and applied fiberglass.

problem: lower cowl r/h rib has delamination.
action: completed fiberglass repair IAW AMM PA28-181 51-70-00.

on post run found the idle to be 980 with no mixture rise, adjusted idle and mixture and couldnt get idle lower than 850 with a 30 mix rise. brought aircraft back in removed cowling. upon inspection found the throttle cable to be hitting its stops on the fuel servo but not getting full travel and found the nut for the bolt that attaches the throttle rod end to fuel servo arm to be damaged. adjusted throttle cable rod end on engine side and removed and replaced the castellated nut on the throttle cabel rod end bolt attachment to fuel servo arm with new. performed ops and travel check for throttle cable, ops and travel check good. reinstalled cowling. ran aircraft through ground run procedures found idle to be 820 with 10 mix rise adjusted idle to 740 with a 30 mix rise. ops check good.

Removed top cowling.  Inspected engine compartment for signs of oil leakage.  noticed the area below the dipstick had excessive oil , possibly from a spill when last serviced.  Cleaned the interior of the cowling and engine.  Performed a run-up and reinspected the engine compartment.  No leakage noted.  Reinstalled top cowling.

problem: Oil door hinge on top cowling broken
no fuel flow indication during engine start up or with any changes to mixture setting. no change with fuel pump turned on or off. no change after engine running.
action: removed top cowl to inspect, found broken wires on fuel flow transducer. removed and replaced fuel flow transducer with new, tightened fuel lines and fittings. ran a/c ops and leak check good. reinstalled top cowl.

problem: L/H LWR Cowl Fire Blanket Damaged
action: Removed damaged part of LH LWR cowl fie blanket and replaced with new.
Applied 2 part epoxy to lower cowl aft fairing crack

problem: During intial compression check. Received reading of 20/80 on cylinder #2
action: Reseated valves on cylinder #2. Compression read 70/80. After runup cylinder #2 EGT read 200-250 degrees lower than others at idle. Performed 2nd compression check. Reading of 20/80. Partially installed top cowl.

problem: During intial compression check. Received reading of 20/80 on cylinder #2
action: Installed new #2 cylinder. Work done IAW Lycoming Direct Drive Overhaul Manual, torqued base nuts, replaced exhaust valve push rod with new. Dry tappet clearance checks good. Reinstalled FWD exhaust stack and #2 intake pipe. Reinstalled #2, #3 and #4 oil return lines. Reinstalled #2 baffle and #2 spark plugs. Torqued plugs to spec. Ops and leak check good. See PD #2 for AD sign off. Installed cowling.

problem: R/H ENGINE ALT AIR HEAT STROUD TABS BROKE
R/H ENGINE ALT AIR HEAT STROUD TABS BROKE
action: REMOVED AND REPLACED ALT. AIR HEAT SHROUD COVER WITH NEW, REINSTALLED COWLING.
Removed and replaced top cowl camloc
Performed fiberglass repair on bottom cowl IAW Piper MM Ch. 51-70-00

problem: Lower cowling center screw is missing
action: Installed new lower cowling center screw.

problem: Lower cowling AFT fairing is broken
action: Removed and replaced lower cowling AFT fairing with new.

problem: Bottom cowling bottom fairing aft attach point need to be built up
action: Built up fiberglass bottom cowl fairing attach points IAW PMM PA 28-181 section 51-70-00

problem: ALTR indicator failure, battery was not being discharged and volts read 28. Ran alt failure checklist to try and reset it but false indication would come on frequently and would not go away
action: Performed full ground run of aircraft in multiple configurations. Could not duplicate dispcrep. ALT reading and battery reading read normal. Removed top cowl and inspected ALT and wiring, no defects noted. Installed top cowl.

problem: No concerns on taxi, 2nd landing in GFK rollout felt like popped tire. (shuttering from left main) taxied back to ramp shuttering sensation remained but reduced at lower speed.
action: Inspected both RH and LH MLG tires and struts. No defects noted. Removed wheel nuts to check for proper installation on both MLG tires no defects found. Reinstalled wheel nuts and recotter pinned. Removed top cowl and check torque of engine mount bolts to firewall. Found top middle bolt to be loose. Torqued bolt to spec. Reinstalled top cowl. Ran A/C found no defects during taxi.

problem: after simulated emergency decent into off airport landing, engine ran rough and started sputtering for 3-5 secs after going full power to recover from the maneuver. after that engine ran fine all the way back to GFK.
action: ran a/c, found idle and mixture settings off, adjusted idle and mixture, ops check good. no other defects found during runup. removed top cowl to inspect engine, no defects found, reinstalled top cowl.

problem: alt fail in cruise. alternator amps were 0 batt volts were dropping and flashing and amps were reading -7. reset alt field cb and positive amperage and voltage returned
action: removed top cowl to inspect alt system, found alt inop fuse discolored, replaced with new fuse. no other defects found. ran a/c ops checks good. alt amps:10, voltage: 28.1, battery amps:0.  reinstalled top cowl.
Stop Drilled Crack in LWR Cowl Aft Fairing and Repaired with Two-Part Epoxy

problem: Upper cowl RH screw tab broken
action: Fabricated and installed new upper cowl RH screw tab

problem: Experienced engine roughness @3500 ft. Conducted engine failure in flight checklist. All engine indications normal. Returned to GFK without incident or any engine abnormalities
action: Ran aircraft again and performed compression check. Determined Cyl #2 has sticky exhaust valve. Boroscoped inside cylinder and determined piston has no damage. Partially installed top cowl

problem: LWR Cowl / NLG Mounting Screw Missing
action: Installed New Screw and Washer in LWR Cowl/ NLG Mounting hole

problem: Multiple reports of engine hot-start failures
Bottom cowl RH screw hole area needs repair
action: Repaired bottom cowl RH screw hole area IAW Piper AMM 51-70-00.

problem: Top cowl Fwd nutplate bracket broken
action: Fabricated top cowl Fwd LH nutplate bracket with .050 2024 T3 and installed. Riveting done IAW Ac 43.13-1B Ch 4-57

problem: pilot reports RH cowling screw loose.
action: tightened cowling screw on RH engine.
Replaced LWR cowl center screw with new
Removed cowl, removed old fuel pump PN 18030-B, SN 210874 and replaced with overhauled fuel pump SN 211549. Ops checked and leak checked. Installed cowl.

problem: RAN UP PLANE AND HAD RPM DROP OF 175 WITH SIGNIFICANT SHAKING, CYL #3 HAD EGT DROP AS WELL, RAN FOULED PLUG CHECKLIST AND ISSUE CONTINUED
action: RAN AIRCRAFT AND VERIFIED DISCREP, FOUND TO BE WHEN SHUTTING L/H MAG OFF CYL #3 EGT WOULD DROP, REMOVED TOP COWLING, REMOVED CYL #3 BOTTOM IGNITION LEAD AND PLUG, CLEANED AND TESTED PLUG, CHECKS GOOD, REINSTALLED CYL #3 BOTTOM PLUG AND IGNITION LEAD, REINSTALLED TOP COWLING, RAN AIRCRAFT OPS AND MAG DROP CHECK GOOD

problem: ALTR FAILURE ON RUNUP, WOULD CONTINUE TO CHIME ANYTIME ABOVE 1100 RPM, ALTR AMPS INDICATED 9-12 THE ENTIRE TIME, RAN CHECKLIST WAS NOT RESOLVED
action: RAN AIRCRAFT VERIFIED DISCREP, REMOVED TOP COWLING INSPECTED WIRING, NO DEFECTS NOTED, REMOVED FUSE AND TESTED, FOUND FUSE TO BE BAD, INSTALLED NEW FUSE, REINSTALLED TOP COWLING, RAN AIRCRAFT OPS CHECK GOOD

problem: LOWER COWL CRACKED AT FWD RH SCREW HOLE
action: PERFORMED COMPOSITE REPAIR TO LOWER COWL FWD RH SCREW HOLE IAW PIPER PA 28-181 MM 51-70

problem: Loosened 2 screws on RH side of lower cowl.
action: Tightened loose screws on RH side of lower cowl.

problem: Engine roughness on upwind, loss of engine RPM, EGT checked good.
action: Reamed #1 and #3 cylinders IAW LYC SI 1425A and performed dry tappet clearance check, clearance check good. Reinstalled cowling and performed ground run-up, ops and leak check good. See WO # 1246180 fo MX flight.

problem: lower cowl fwd l/h cam lock collar worn.
action: removed and replaced lower cowl fwd l/h cam lock and recepticle with new.

problem: Right engine prop malfunction. When decreasing RPM it would not stay at set RPM instead slowly increase to max RPM. Also on landing-both props full the right engine RPM was at 2250 rather than 2550 like the left engine when both  props were set to high RPM
action: Performed ground run and could not duplicate discrepancy. Removed LH and RH top cowlings to inspect engines, No defects noted reinstalled LH and RH top cowlings. See UND WKO# 1246145 for MX flight.

problem: Found fuild on the nose wheel while moving aircraft. Looked and couldn't find source of leak.- UND Line
action: "Inspected aircraft and found a small amount of oil on the cowling and nose wheel assembly. Oil appears consistent with spillage during recent servicing. Noted minor oil discharge from engine breather. Performed leak checkÂ—no active leaks observed.
Finished ENG install except for installing ENG cowling. Performed ground run and found idle to be low and mixture rise to be high, adjusted idle and mixture rise to spec. Ops and leak check.

problem: In flight, alternator failure. Battery amps negitive and alternator apms 0. No C/B popped. Ran checklist, alternator still failed. Retutned to GFk.
action: Removed cowling. Found the alternator power wire terminal broken, replaced with new alternator power wire terminal. Found alternator power terminal post to be burnt, removed and replaced with new alternator P/N 602-483 S/N H-Z093391 and installed new alternator fuse. Ran aircraft alternator failed again, Removed and replaced with new alternator inop switch and another new alternator fuse. Ops check good

problem: lower cowl rib l/h and r/h fiberglass damged.
action: cleaned up damaged fiberglass on lower cowl l/h and r/h sides of rib and applied fiberglass. completed fiberglass repair IAW AMM PA28-181 51-70-00.

problem: Top cowl FWD LH screw plate broken
action: Removed top cowl found screw plate broken on LH side. Fabricated new top cowl FWD screw plate out of 2024 T3 .063 aluminium. Installed screw plate onto cowl. Reinstalled top cowl.

problem: Top cowling RH forward nutplate missing.
action: Replaced the top cowling RH forward nutplate.

problem: Flight crew requested MX assistance after 5 failed engine hot-start attempts, reporting slow propeller rotation.
action: Removed starter, P/N 149NL/EC S/N H-Z062042. Installed new starter P/N 149NL/EC S/N H-Z083384. Ops checked good. Installed cowling.

problem: "During engine start when we turned on the fuel pump it was making a weird nose, after about 30 seconds it completely stopped making nose."
action: Removed top cowling and inspected electric fuel pump. Operated fuel pump with no defects noted. Installed top cowling.

problem: Bottom cowling aft nut plate broken
action: repaired fiber glass on bottom cowl L/H aft fairing attach point IAW PMM PA-28-181 section 51-70-00 and installed nut plate, Riveted IAW AC 43.13-1B. section 4-57
Removed top cowling. Inspected engine after break-in flight.  Reinstalled top cowling.
Removed top cowling.  Inspected engine after break-in flight.  Reinstalled top cowling.
replaced engine mount nuts with new, torqued nuts. engine PN: IO-360-B4A SN: L-39027-51E installed engine cowling.

problem: R/H bottom cowling oil door striker plate worn out and rivets loose
action: Instaled new oil door striker plate on the R/H bottom cowl. Fit up and function test with oil door. Ops checked good.
Applied 2 part epoxy to top cowl fiberglass delamination

problem: middle screw missing on lower cowl under nlg fairing.
action: installed new screw on lower cowl under nlg fairing.
REMOVED TOP COWLING AND SERVICED NLG STRUT WITH N2, REINSTALLED TOP COWLING

problem: Upper cowl fiberglass getting weak near RH inlet.
action: started fiberglass repair on upper cowl near RH inlet IAW PMM PA 28-181 section 51-70-00.
Removed top cowling.  Replaced cylinder #1 EGT probe.  Operational check good.  Reinstalled top cowling.

problem: A785-11 Hose due for time change (Engine L/H cowling to airbox)
A785-11 Hose due for time change (Engine L/H cowling to airbox)
l/h engine top cowl oil door hinge pin migrated.
action: Reset LH Eng top cowl oil door hinge pin
Installed new lower cowl center screw

problem: RH Eng top cowl needs 2 camlocs replaced
action: Removed and replaced 2 camlocs on RH Eng top cowl

problem: Cabin heat not noticed entire flight.
action: Removed top cowl and inspected all heater hoses, no defects noted. Installed cowl and performed ground run on A/C, verified heat coming from vents in cabin throughout ground run.

problem: pilot reports oil stain on LH side of bottom engine cowling.
action: removed top engine cowl and inspected, no causes of oil leak found, determined oil stain to be from prior maintenance, cleaned cowling and reinstalled top cowl. no defects found.

problem: "While checking oil, where the oil dipstick screws in is loose and at the base there is some oil. Tube loose, tighten and safety required."
action: Removed top engine cowling.  Removed dipstick tube and installed new gasket.  Reinstalled and safetied dipstick tube.  Reinstalled top engine cowling.

problem: A portion RH lower cowl heat shielding is coming up.
action: Removed and replaced a portion of RH lower cowl heat shielding.

problem: Engine misses when checking the LH magneto.
action: Removed cowling. Removed L/H ignition harness and installed new L/H ignition harness. Performed engine ground run with no defects noted. Installed cowling.

problem: "No RPM drop during runup when alternate air was opened."
action: Removed cowling, found alt air cable broken. Removed alternate airbox, cleaned, inspected, and reinstalled. Installed new alt air control cable and connected to airbox. Ops checked good. Installed cowling.
Removed and replaced 2 camlocs on top cowl
Installed missing lower cowl center screw

problem: RH brake mushy. was almost fully in during landing rolls.
action: Removed top cowl for access. Bled RH brakes and parking brake. Ops and leak check good.

problem: RH brake mushy. was almost fully in during landing rolls.
action: REINSTALLED TOP COWLING REMOVED FOR ACCESS
REMOVED AND REPLACED COWLING CAM LOC

problem: Called line for oil, and cap of oil bottle potentially fell in engine cowling. Was not found outside of aircraft or in engine cowling during quick look from maintenance.
action: Found and removed oil bottle cap from LWR cowling. No other FOD found.

problem: WINTER PLATES NEED TO BE INSTALLED
action: REMOVED TOP COWLINGS, INSTALLED WINTER PLATES, REINSTALLED TOP COWLINGS

problem: WINTER PLATES NEED TO BE INSTALLED

action: Removed top cowling.  Serviced nose strut with nitrogen. Reinstalled top cowling.

problem: "Fouled spark plug. 270 drop. Checklist didn't work."
action: Both LH and RH magnetos operational check good during ground engine run.  Reinstalled engine cowling.

problem: "Rapid RPM drop and engine roughness during runup"
action: Removed top cowling and identified #1 cylinder bottom spark plug was fouled.  Cleaned, inspected and tested spark plug.  Reinstalled cleaned bottom spark plug with a good operational check on ground engine run.  Reinstalled top cowling.

problem: pilot reports oil streak on LH lower cowl.
action: removed top cowl to inspect engine, no defects found. oil streak found to be from prior maintenance. reinstalled top cowl and cleaned streak.

problem: pilot reports top cowl camlock loose
oil door latch broke off on preflight. the silver latch dropped inside the cowling to the bottom.
action: removed and replaced oil door latch with new, ops check good. riveting done IAW AC43.13-1b, section 4-57

problem: Reduced power when entering the pattern, oil PSI dropped to 40 and got a caution. Aflter landing and power idle , oil PSI dropped to 23 PSI and we got a red warning. Taxing back, PSI increased as throttle increased and when we parked it went back to 25 PSI
action: Ran aircraft and could not duplicate discrepancy. Removed cowlings and drained oil, cut open oil filter, inspected and cleaned oil sump screen and oil pressure relief valve. Installed new oil filter and reinstalled oil sump screen and oil pressure relief valve and resafetied. Refilled engine with oil. Used barfield gage to test oil pressure indicator and found indicator to be accurate, reinstalled cowlings. Performed ground run of aircraft and found oil pressure to be 60 PSI at idle(oil temp was 160 Fahrenheit). No defects noted, Ops and leak check good.
Tightened loose camlock on upper LH side of cowl.

problem: LH ENG upper cowl camlock loose.
action: Tightened loose camlock on LH ENG.

problem: ENG died multiple times on touchdown, able to restart ENG every time it died but had to keep ENG RPM above 1500 to keep the ENG from dying.
action: Ran up aircraft and was able to duplicate discrep. Removed upper and lower cowl and partially removed ALT air box. Ordered parts.

problem: ENG died multiple times on touchdown, able to restart ENG every time it died but had to keep ENG RPM above 1500 to keep the ENG from dying.
action: ran a/c and made idle/mixture adjustments, ops check good. installed top and bottom cowling, MX flight required see UND WO# 1246990

problem: Camlock on upper cowl on the LH side won't lock.
action: Removed and replaced camlock on upper cowl on LH side that wouldn't lock. New camlock locks good.

problem: Oil puddle found under plane when parked in wash bay.
action: Removed upper and lower cowl and washed engine and cowling. Performed ground run and leak check and found oil coming from oil cooler. Removed and replaced oil cooler with PN: 20710A SN: D22-14959-11. Installed lower cowl. Performed ground run up. Ops and leak check good.

problem: Lycoming SI 1425A MX procedure to reduce valve sticking
Forward L/H Top Cowl Camlock Loose.
action: Removed And Replaced Forward L/H Top Cowl Camlock Recepticle.

problem: lower cowl air inlet for cabin heat loose.
action: removed and replaced loose rivets holding the inlet on lower cowl for cabin heat with new. ops check good.

problem: lower cowl heat shield needs to be glued back down.
action: Glued lower cowl heat shield back in place.

problem: Loosened screw on RH side of the lower cowl.
action: Tightened loose screw on RH side on lower cowl.

problem: 2 camloc rings popped off on R/H side top cowl
action: Re installed 2 camloc retaining ring on R/H side of top cowl

problem: 200+ RPM drop on the RH magneto during run-up
action: Removed top engine cowling.  Removed the bottom spark plug from cylinder #1, found a large lead/carbon deposit.  Cleaned and tested spark plug.  Reinstalled spark plug into bottom position of cylinder #1.  Operational check good.  Reinstalled top engine cowling.

problem: RPM dropped greater than 250 RPM on LH magneto.

Cleaned and tested spark plug, Reinstalled spark plug into bottom position of cylinder #1.  Operational check good.  Reinstalled top cowling.

problem: pilot reports bottom cowling NLG fairing screw loose.

problem: r/h and l/h lower engine cowls heat shielding damaged.
action: Removed damaged heat shielding and installed new on RH lower cowling

problem: r/h and l/h lower engine cowls heat shielding damaged.
r/h and l/h lower engine cowls heat shielding damaged.

action: Removed LH engine cowling.  Inspected LH alternator and found the output post ring terminal broken.  Replaced ring terminal.  Installed new GMW5 fuse.  Operational  check good.  Reinstalled engine cowling.
Replaced NLG strut rear lower cowling fairing with new part. Drilled, trimed, and painted new fairing.

problem: "During the cold start up check list the prop stopped turning even with the starter switch pressed. Tried the engine flooded checklist but the starter does not turn prop when the starter switch is pressed."
action: Removed engine cowling and removed starter. Disassembled starter and replaced shear pin with new Ref. PA-28-181 MM 80-10-00, paragraph 3.F. Reassembled starter and reinstalled onto engine. Operational check good. Reinstalled engine cowling.

problem: Cowling nut plate on NLG missing
action: Installed new cowling nut plate on NLG.

problem: Cowling nut plate on NLG missing

action: Installed screw in LH engine FWD lower cowl

action: Removed engine cowling on RH engine and inspected alternator wiring, found field wire for RH alternator to have a bad connection. Crimped new terminal end on field wire. Reinstalled cowling and performed ground run, Ops check good.

problem: Alt fail on cruise -> ran checklist. No fix. turned around and came back alt amp was zero
action: Ran aircraft confirmed issue, removed Cowl found idler pulley to be damaged, removed and replaced idler pulley found belt to be good and no other areas of concern tensioned belt to within spec. reinstalled cowl.

problem: While doing run up we checked the alt air and we had no drop.
action: Inspected and found alt air cable broken. Removed alternate airbox, cleaned and installed new upper and lower grommets. Installed new alternate airbox cable. Installed new air box swivel and stud assy. Reinstalled alternate airbox and safetied hardware. Ops checked good. Reinstalled engine cowling.

problem: In the PA, we got ALTR fail. Bat amps= -13  ALT amps= 0. We ran ALT failure checklist. ALT came back for 10 seconds failed again
action: Performed ground run and verified discrepancy. Removed top cowling and found to be no power going to F1 terminal.

problem: In the PA, we got ALTR fail. Bat amps= -13  ALT amps= 0. We ran ALT failure checklist. ALT came back for 10 seconds failed again
action: Removed lower cowl and inspected the wire running from the alternator F1 terminal to the voltage regulator, no defects noted. Removed and replaced voltage regulator S/N 80W57904, ALT INOP switch and alternator F102 fuse with new. Installed lower and upper cowl. Performed ground run of aircraft, ALT ops check good.

problem: Engine break-in flight required; REF. WO# 1247369
action: Removed top cowling.  Inspected engine after break-in flight.  Reinstalled top cowling.

problem: Engine has 3,006.0 hours TSO; 5,443.4 hours TT
action: Removed engine cowling. Removed RH wire harness. Removed RH baffles. Disconnected fuel and oil lines. Disconnected throttle and mixture rod ends.

problem: "During runup we tested mags. When we turned the right mag off, the left magneto had an abrupt drop of 120 rpm, compared to the slow 40 rpm drop of the right mag. We ran the faulty plug check and the engine was misfiring on the left mag when leaned to peak EGT."
action: Started engine and performed run up. When running on the left mag, the engine ran rough and the cylinder #4 EGT dropped. Removed top engine cowling and removed lower cylinder #4 spark plug. Cleaned, tested and re-installed spark plug. Mag check good. Re-installed top engine cowling.

problem: R/H engine cylinder #1 EGT is yellow X
action: Installed new EGT probe on R/H engine cylinder #1. Ops check good, reinstalled cowling.

problem: L ALT amps showed zero after takeoff, R ALT amps showed 20 . verified both alt switched were on. increased electrical load, but L alt still zero. Ran single alternator failure checklist and alt came back on. Returned to GFK. no alt fail
action: Removed top cowl found L ALT belt to be loose, checked belt for defects no defects found, tightened belt to with in spec and safetied, ops check good Reinstalled L/H top cowl
Inspected engine cowling. No defects observed.

problem: During preflight noticed one of the screws was able to be rotated in and out if you are looking at the plane head on. it is on the R/H side, in the top corner
action: Went out to aircraft could not find loose screw, double checked all cowl screws and camlocs
Installed lower left cowl screw and washer

problem: On run up noticed RH ENG oil pressure was slightly lower than LH ENG(65 PSI compared to 80 PSI) On climb out oil pressure continued to drop to 56-58 PSI. Ran low oil pressure checklist at 4500 ft with no success. upon landing oil pressure fell below 20 PSI at idle and 30 PSI on taxi.
action: Performed ground run on aircraft and verified discrep. Removed top cowl and panels to gain access to RH ENG oil transducer and inspected ENG visually, no defects noted. Performed barfield test, ops check good. Removed all upper spark plugs on RH ENG and performed compression test, every CYL passed the compression test. Installed and torqued all upper spark plugs on RH ENG.

Removed top engine cowling and found alternator field wire broken at the ring terminal.  Installed new ring terminal on field wire at the alternator.  Performed ground engine run with good operational check.  Reinstalled top engine cowling.

problem: Pilot reported oil by engine cowling
action: Inspected engine and found oil to be from a spill during oil servicing. Cleaned engine and engine cowling

problem: On the climb out of GFK, felt engine roughness(slight jolt/bump) when changing power setting, Engine parameters remained normal as far as I checked.
action: Removed top cowl and inspected engine, no defects noted. Ran aircraft, could not duplicate disrepency. No defects noted. Reinstalled top cowl
Removed engine cowling.  Inspected alternator charging system. Found a broken ring terminal on the output wire.  Installed new ring terminal on the output wire.
Installed wiring to back of alternator and starter, safetied all bolts removed for repair, installed and tensioned alternator belt, and installed cowling

problem: RH ALT fail after engine start. we ran the checklist and the annunciator came back.
action: Ran up aircraft and verified discrep. Removed upper and lower cowl and the cowl chins on the RH ENG. Inspected RH alternator wiring, no defects noted. Removed and replaced RH ALT fuse with new. Installed lower and upper cowl and cowl chins on RH ENG. Ran up aircraft, ops check good on RH alternator.

problem: during our pattern flight on the downwind we had a sudden loss of power and engine roughness. all indications were in the green arc. on postflight oil was observed on the side of the cowling. no indications on runup prior to takeoff /taxi.
action: removed top cowl, found #1 injection line broke at weld, ordered new line.

problem: during our pattern flight on the downwind we had a sudden loss of power and engine roughness. all indications were in the green arc. on postflight oil was observed on the side of the cowling. no indications on runup prior to takeoff /taxi.
action: Removed and replaced #1 injection line with new. Ran fuel pump with throttle and mixture levers full forward. Leak checks good.

problem: during our pattern flight on the downwind we had a sudden loss of power and engine roughness. all indications were in the green arc. on postflight oil was observed on the side of the cowling. no indications on runup prior to takeoff /taxi.
action: Ran A/C ops and leak check good

problem: LH engine failure on landing roll out
action: Removed top cowl and inspected engine, no defects noted. Ran aircraft, no defects noted. Reinstalled top cowl

problem: L/H Side top cowl cam loc needs to be replaced
action: REMOVED AND REPLACED L/H SIDE TOP COWLING CAM LOC

problem: top cowl r/h fwd latch plate rivets loose.
action: Drilled out rivets of top cowl RH FWD latch plate and replaced with screws, nuts and washers.

problem: Found strip hanging out of LH engine cowling on post flight.
action: Found portion of cowling seal loose on LH engine upper cowl, Trimmed and secured loose seal portion.

problem: R/H engine cylinder #1 EGT fluctuates and then goes unreadable
action: Removed top cowling and found R/H engine cylinder #1 EGT probe terminal loose. Tightened and reconnected EGT terminal. Reinstalled top cowling. Operational check good.
Removed and replaced lower cowl forward bottom fairing, stop drilled cracks on lower cowl aft bottom fairing and applied 2 part epoxy
Removed top cowling.  Inspected alternator and wires.   Found the alternator inoperative fuse burnt out, replaced the alt inop fuse with new.  Operational check good.  Reinstalled top cowling.
Installed chafe tape on top cowling.

problem: Bottom Engine Cowling Center Screw Missing.
action: Installed Bottom Engine Cowling Center Screw With New.
Fabricated top cowl nutplate bracket with .050 2024 T3 and installed. Riveting done IAW AC 43.13-1B Ch. 4-57

problem: OB Stabilator trim tab hinges worn
action: Ordered parts, and put them on cowl rack

problem: Right side oil temp got to 247 degrees for 5-10 seconds during OEI ops. Got it back to normal but was high for the entire flight. Cowl flaps closed, OAT +2
action: Removed top cowling on right engine and inspected engine, no defects noted. Reinstalled right engine top cowling. Performed ground run of aircraft and could not duplicate discrepancy. Checks good.

problem: Upper engine cowling fastener is loose
action: Tightened fastener on upper engine cowling

problem: During the run up right magneto dropped 110 RPM left magneto only dropped 50 RPM. Unfortunatly forgot to check cylinder head temp to see which cylinder head temp dropped the greatest.
action: Performed ground run and confirmed discrepancy, removed cowlings and found RH mag timing to be off. Retimed RH mag to engine, reinstalled cowlings and performed post run. Ops and leak check good.

problem: Pilot reports loose screw on cowl.
action: Secured camloc fastener on left side of cowl.

problem: Pilot reports loose screw on cowl.
action: Secured camloc fastener on left side of cowl.

problem: Top cowl needs chafe tape applied
action: Applied chafe tape to top cowling.

problem: R/H Cowl lock lever not working properly
action: Removed and Replaced R/H Cowl Lock Lever With New. Ops Check Good.

problem: CHT 400 degrees during the start on slow flight. Start climb and CHT went up to 470-472 degrees. High oil temp cas message. Ran high oil temp checklist and left and left cowl flaps open during return to GFK. CHT stayed below 400 degrees after checklist was completed.
action: Removed L/H and R/H top cowlings. inspected L/H and R/H engines. no defects notes. Installed L/H and R/H top cowlings. Found when running an airplane with cowl flaps closed the engines will run hot due to no airflow.

problem: CAS warning message. low voltage. alternator Amps indicating 0.
action: Removed cowling. found alternator bracket arm bolt missing. Inspected alternator, no damage found. Installed new alternator bracket arm bolt with new hardware and safety wired new bolt. Installed cowling and ran aircraft. ops check good

problem: Top cowl removed for 102 class display
Oil door on upper cowl will not latch.
action: Recontoured oil door on upper cowl. Oil door latches and un-latches properly.

problem: Holding short of the runway, metal-on-metal sound heard(starter engaged). Smelled like melted wire, but no fire or smoke observed. Taxied back under own power with no issues.
action: Removed upper and lower cowl. Found alternator F102 fuse and fuse cap to be burned, removed and replaced alternator F102 fuse and fuse cap with new. Found starter ground cable to be rubbing on exhaust intake scat tube, no damage noted on starter ground wire. Added chafe tape to starter ground cable. Found starter solenoid to be stuck in the open position. Ordered new starter solenoid. Starter INOP from starter solenoid being stuck open, removed and replaced starter with new P/N 149NL/EC S/N H-Z070610.

problem: Bottom cowling center screw is missing
action: Installed new bottom cowling center screw.

problem: On run up, leaned to peak with RH ENG side dropping out of standards. Re-leaned multiple times with no fix. Ran fouled plug checklist, RH ENG tried to quit.
action: Performed ground run of A/C. LH ENG and RH ENG mag drops were both within spec. RH ENG and LH ENG ran smooth the whole runup, neither ENG wanted to die.  Removed RH ENG top cowl and visually inspected ENG, no defects noted. Installed top cowl on RH ENG.

problem: Bottom cowling center screw is missing
action: Installed new bottom cowling center screw.

problem: Bottom cowling cabin heat air inlet fastners loose
action: Removed and replaced bottom cowling cabin heat air inlet loose fasteners with new.

problem: Bottom cowl reinforcement piece R/H side loose
action: Started layup fiberglass on bottom cowl repair

problem: Bottom cowl reinforcement piece R/H side loose
action: Completed layup repair on Bottom cowl R/H side IAW PMM PA-28-181 section 51-70-00

problem: Pilot reported oil dripping from cowling
action: Found clean oil dripping from engine cowling to be from serving engine oil. After further inspection found funnel cracked that was used for servicing. Cleaned engine and cowling.

problem: Pilot reported oil dripping for engine cowling
action: FOUND CLEAN OIL DRIPPING FROM ENGINE COWLING TO BE FROM SERVICING ENGINE OIL. AFTER FURTHER INSPECTION FOUND FUNNEL CRACKED THAT WAS USED FOR SERVICING. CLEANED ENGINE AND COWLING

problem: lower cowl left side fiberglass broke at fwd screw hole
action: top cowl removed for lower cowl repair

problem: lower cowl left side fiberglass broke at fwd screw hole
action: removed damaged area, feathered area, laid in new fiberglass and resin, primed and painted, located screw hole, work accomplished referencing MM 51-70-00 para 1A and B

problem: lower cowl left side fiberglass broke at fwd screw hole
On run-up alternate air increased RPM on LH ENG by 50.
action: Removed top cowl on LH ENG and inspected LH ENG, no defects noted. Installed LH ENG top cowl. Performed ground run of aircraft and found LH and RH ALT air drops to be in spec.

problem: L/H mixture behaves strangely, peak EGT in cruise is around 80% mixture. at that point increasing mixture reduces fuel flow by about 0.5 GPH and leaning to mixture increases fuel flow by the same amount. CHT's normal. Fuel pump on has not effect
action: Removed LH engine cowling. Removed and replaced LH engine fuel servo with overhauled. PN: AV2581700 SN: AV54769016. Ran A/C, adjusted idle and mixture rise. Ops and leak check good. Reinstalled LH engine cowling.

problem: On run up alt air indicated a 70 drop in the open position and remained at 1950 when closed. open position was attempted multiple times, no rpm changes indicated.
action: Removed cowl, found alt air box stud and swivel missing. Installed new stud and swivel. Travel checks good. Reinstalled cowling.

Removed L/H cowling and aft cowling. Removed airframe L/H mag adjusted and cleaned engine tach points. reinstalled airframe L/H mag with new gaskets and timed to engine. Installed new oil filter and installed L/H and aft cowling

problem: Departing KPKD noticed high EGT cylinder #1 at 1750-1900 all others indicating normal readings would intermittanly cutout during flight back. Oil temp and cyl #2,3,4 remained normall throughout the flight.
action: Ran A/C could not duplicate high EGT on cyl #1. Removed top cowl no defects noted. Reinstalled top cowl.

problem: Used the heater for the flight. First half of flight it was fine. Then it stopped working and we couldn't feel any heat for the rest of the flight.
action: Removed top cowl and inspected heater components. Checked insides of scat tubing for any FOD no defects noted. Heater valve operated normally. Reinstalled top cowl.
